title: Beat
description: Validates a beat artifact — the grounded scope and assessed source map for one Gazette issue. Produced by survey; consumed by report, edit, and publish.

issue_id.description: Echoes brief.issue_id — the issue this beat scopes.

scope.description: The grounded, bounded scope of this issue — refined from brief.scope_intent against what the corpus actually supports.

corpus_map.description: The reachable historical sources, each assessed for the weight it can bear. Includes both sources named in the brief and sources discovered by following trails outward.

inherited_threads.description: Open threads carried forward from the prior issue's ledger. Empty for the first issue.

corpus_gaps.description: Parts of the intended scope the corpus does not support — honest gaps. A gap marked materially relevant is a candidate for archive-gap reporting downstream.

locator.description: Where the source is — a path, URL, or reference.

description.description: What the source is and what it covers.

kind.description: Optional classification of the source — e.g. git-history, issue-tracker, orientation-doc, deployment-record.

assessment.description: Practical quality context so downstream protocols can judge how much weight this source can bear.

discovered_from.description: When this source was reached by following a trail rather than named in the brief, the locator of the source that led here. Makes discovered trails auditable.

reliability.description: primary = an authoritative first-hand record (a commit, a tracker entry); secondary = a derived or summarizing record; unverified = provenance not established.

freshness.description: How current the source is relative to the issue's scope window.

completeness.description: How completely the source covers what it purports to cover.

ownership.description: Who owns or authored the source, when known — e.g. a repository, an account, a system.

description.description: What part of the intended scope the corpus cannot support.

material.description: True when the gap is materially relevant to the chronicle — significant enough to be worth reporting as an archive-gap story rather than merely noted.

gap_evidence.description: When material, the evidence that the gap exists — e.g. a development visible in one source with no corresponding record in another.

thread_id.description: Stable identifier for the thread across issues.

origin_issue.description: issue_id where the thread was opened.
